name: ExtensionTestScript_Setup_Action_Assert_DefaultManualCompletion
title: R5: fail | pass | skip | stop (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `TestScript.setup.action.assert.defaultManualCompletion` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`TestScript.setup.action.assert.defaultManualCompletion` 0..1 `code`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `TestScript.setup.action.assert.defaultManualCompletion` 0..1 `code`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `TestScript.setup.action.assert.defaultManualCompletion` has a context of TestScript.setup.action.assert based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `TestScript`.
Element `TestScript.setup.action.assert.defaultManualCompletion` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:
Element `TestScript.setup.action.assert.defaultManualCompletion` has a context of TestScript.setup.action.assert based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `TestScript`.
Element `TestScript.setup.action.assert.defaultManualCompletion` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Manual completion is used to pause the test engine execution and evaluation allowing an external review of the defined assert condition. The defaultManualCompletion defines the default manual completion outcome applied if one of the enumerated values is not applied.

element.definition: The default manual completion outcome applied to this assertion.
element.comment: Manual completion is used to pause the test engine execution and evaluation allowing an external review of the defined assert condition. The defaultManualCompletion defines the default manual completion outcome applied if one of the enumerated values is not applied.

binding.description: The default type of manual completion to use for assertion.
